description_long: This is a classic stovetop espresso maker, commonly known as a moka pot, with a faceted, octagonal body and a matte black finish. The upper chamber is open, revealing the spout and the central column where brewed coffee rises. The lid is hinged and partially open, featuring a knob for easy lifting. A curved, ergonomic handle is attached to the side, made from a material that appears to be heat-resistant. The lower chamber is also faceted and screws onto the upper part, with a small safety valve visible on the side. The overall design is utilitarian yet stylish, suitable for brewing coffee on a stovetop in urban kitchens or cafes.
description: A matte black, faceted stovetop espresso maker (moka pot) with an open lid, ergonomic handle, and visible safety valve. The design is both functional and stylish, ideal for brewing coffee.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open lid, spout, and the central column inside the upper chamber.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open lid, ergonomic handle, and the faceted body; the safety valve is visible on the lower chamber.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the handle's attachment and the closed back of the lid, with the knob centered.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the open lid from a different angle and the handle's curvature.
